text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, simpler, and easier to understand. When developers refactor, they remove duplication, rename confusing variables, or break long functions into smaller ones. This does not add new features, but it pays off a kind of "technical debt"—the hidden cost of messy code. When left unpaid, technical debt slows down future changes. By regularly refactoring, teams keep the codebase healthy. This improves readability, making it easier for others to read, fix, or extend the software later.
